faq.question: How much does window replacement cost in Mountain View?
faq.answer: Mountain View homeowners typically invest $7,000 to $16,000 for a full window replacement project covering 10–16 windows. This Silicon Valley tech hub — home to Google — features a range of mid-century ranch homes and contemporary residences where energy performance and modern aesthetics are priorities. Single window installation starts at approximately $600.

faq.question: Do I need a permit for window replacement in Mountain View?
faq.answer: Yes. Mountain View requires a building permit for window replacements. Our team handles all permit applications with the City of Mountain View Community Development Department on your behalf.

faq.question: Which window brands does Deluxe Windows install in Mountain View?
faq.answer: Mountain View's tech-forward Silicon Valley market drives demand for energy-efficient, high-performance products. We most frequently install Anlin Del Mar triple-pane for maximum solar heat gain control, Andersen 400-Series for mid-century and Eichler homes, and Milgard Tuscany vinyl for cost-effective whole-home replacements.

faq.question: Does Deluxe Windows serve all Mountain View neighborhoods?
faq.answer: Yes — we serve all Mountain View neighborhoods including Cuernavaca, Monta Loma, Waverly Park, Stierlin Estates, and Old Mountain View. Zip codes: 94040, 94041, 94043.
